reasoning: The index baseline is established at 3416.29 (Feb 28). This value represents a significant short-term rally in bonds (10-year yield fell to 4.24%). As a result, short-term mean reversion makes it more likely than not that the baseline will not be exceeded in the very near term (Mar 9, Apr 1), supported by the FRED tool's trend projection of 3410 and historical seasonal probability around 0.33-0.37. Over longer horizons, the ~5% annualized yield of the US Corporate index provides roughly a 14-point monthly upward drift (coupon clip). This secular drift increasingly outpaces typical yield fluctuations, driving the probability of positive variance above 0.50 for the 3-month, 6-month, and 1-year marks. Consequently, probabilities form an upward-sloping term structure reflecting initial rate reversion risk followed by steady deterministic yield accumulation. (Note: Total return indices conceptually violate the generic heuristic that 'longer distance = closer to 0.5' because the deterministic drift increasingly dominates the noise over time.)
